raw_consumer_statement:
I was in XXXX on a short family vacation when on XX/XX/XXXX  a cab driver picked me up on the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. He dropped me at my hotel on the XXXX XXXX XXXX ( a short ride that probably should have cost around {$14.00} ). I tried to pay with my XXXX XXXX XXXX, but he told me it didn't work. I then have him my Citibank XXXX and the charge went through. I did not get a receipt. 
When I got back to my room about 10 minutes later, I saw a fraud alert from XXXX. I immediately texted my husband at home in XXXX, New Jersey and asked him to call XXXX. I then saw the charge on my XXXX of {$3500.00} made for consulting services in XXXX, XXXX. 
I then asked my husband to call XXXX as well. Both credit card companies shut down the card and sent new ones to my home. XXXX XXXX took the charge off my account immediately, but I can not get XXXX to do the same. 
XXXX is insistent that I should have filed a police report, but I did not know the driver 's name, nor his ID or the car 's license plate -- I had no way to identify him. The card was in my possession, a fact which seems to work against me. I have been a Citibank customer for more than 40 years with an excellent creidt rating and I feel as if I am being treated as a dishonest person or cheat.
